title: Prefetch, Consumer Concurrency, Capacity, and Fairness · Contract Lens: Adaptive Prefetch Caution
summary: A consumer sets prefetch zero for maximum speed, starts unbounded promises, ignores message bytes and dependency pools, and scales consumers from queue depth alone. Planned artifact: Add and verify the adaptive prefetch caution increment in prefetch, concurrency, and capacity budget model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Change prefetch only with bounded measurement windows, hysteresis, safe limits, in-flight accounting, and rollback evidence.
Interpret consumer capacity with delivery rate, processing latency, ready and unacked depth, dependency saturation, and queue single-core limits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Adaptive Prefetch Caution · Theory Adaptive Prefetch Caution
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 6882 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to combine prefetch, active handlers, downstream capacity, and message-byte budgets; retain duplicate-safe identity through every ambiguous handoff; then add one subscriber responsibility without changing publisher intent. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Adaptive Prefetch Caution contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Adaptive Prefetch Caution · Theory Adaptive Prefetch Caution.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Build Adaptive Prefetch Caution evidence · Theory Adaptive Prefetch Caution
steps.instruction:
